segs.utf8:
-Welcome to the show.
Thank you for being here.
I want to get into everything.

I want to talk about
your record, by the way,

debuting number three
on the Billboard chart.

Not too shabby!
-Come on!

[ Cheers and applause ]
-Come on! Not too shabby!
"Luck...or Something."

I want to talk about you
playing Madison Square Garden.

I want to talk about you
doing all this stuff.

First off, how's the fam?
How's everybody?

-Oh, my God.
Chaotic as ever, but the best.

-Yeah. That's it.
-We're having fun.

-Yeah. What are they --
What are they into now?
What do they watch at home?

-Oh, well,
you know, we have spans.

We have a teenager,
and then we have a 2-year-old,
and two in between.

So it's a lot of different
things.

It depends on the time of day,
really.

-Okay.
-In the morning
we're heavy into "Bluey."

-Yeah.
That's an unbelievable show.
-It's a great show.

Honestly, sometimes I feel like
it's soothing for our teenager.

He's like --
gets kind of enthralled in it.

Like, I'm like, I get it.
It's like peaceful.

-Oh, I watch it
when the kids aren't home.

-Jimmy --
-Put that one on again.
That one made me cry.

-"Peppa Pig" can
really get under my skin.
-Oh. Okay. Too much?

-It's not that it's not
a sweet show.
It's just like...

-You've been through it.
-You know, in your family,

do you like --
I'm like, "Oh, Daddy's going to

take you to school.
Oh brother's here. Oh, like --"

And so every time
"Peppa Pig" is playing,

I just hear "Brother George!
Daddy Pig!"

-Daddy Pig!
-Daddy Pig!

And I don't think
Matt appreciates that.

-No, of course
he does not appreciate that.

-You know?
-No.

-Those accents just live
in my head from "Peppa Pig."

-Everyone in your family,
though, are they musicians?

Because I know you are
and your husband is.

-Yeah. I mean, as much as
4- and 7-year-olds think that --

They're not like savants
by any means,
but they can really, you know,

they can really get down
to some like KATSEYE

and some "KPop Demon Hunter."
-Yes.

-Yeah.
-And do they listen to --
Do they listen to Mom's music?

-They love my music.
-Yeah.

-It's very --
[ Cheers and applause ]
It's very sweet.

I think that, you know, if
Matt and I get ahold of, like,

the -- You guys,
we play music on like Alexa.

It's so dorky.
-Me too.

-Okay, great. So we just played
on Alexa,

and if Matt and I
get to it first,

you know, we'll put on
like Squeeze or, like,

Jackson Browne and like
Counting Cr--
like, anything that we love

and grew up with
and try to instill.
And if we put it on first,

they'll just listen
and not be requesting.

Like, I think they're just happy
that something's on.
But if we don't get to it first,

it's straight up, like,
for the whole entire day...

-K-pop?
-Yeah.

Or like...
♪ It's raining tacos ♪

-Yeah! Oh, my gosh!
Yes, "It's Raining Tacos."

-It's absolutely horrific.
-God, that always comes on.

-Right?
-Yes.

-Or ♪ Chicken wing,
chicken wing ♪
♪ Hot dog and bologna ♪

It's just...
-♪ Da-da-da-da-da ♪ Yeah.

-Right?
-Yeah.

-I love...
-♪ Hot dog and bologna ♪

-Yeah. It's not okay.
But you know what?
Like, love KATSEYE, love...

-What was the other one
that I used to actually love?
It was like, uh...
♪ Go Pizza Hut, go, Pizza Hut ♪

♪ Kentucky Fried Chicken
and a pizza ♪

-That was our era.
-Oh.

-No?
-Yeah, maybe.

-Wait, hold on, hold on,
hold on.
-♪ McDonald's, McDonald's ♪

-♪ Kentucky Fried Chicken
and a Pizza Hut ♪
-♪ McDonald's, McDonald's ♪

♪ Kentucky Fried Chicken
and a Pizza Hut ♪

-Why are we here?
What are we doing?
How did we get here?

Because now I have the Hot
Pocket song stuck in my head,

and I worked so hard as
a child to remember the crispy,
crunchy, tender, flaky crust.

-Yeah.
-You remember that?

Nailed it. Wow.
-It's still in there.

-Yeah.
-Do you think that?

Do you think that they're like,
"What tangent are they on?"

-No.
-None of these things.

-Everyone -- I have so many
things.

They're just, like,
ask you about "Lizzie McGuire,"

is what they're saying.
-Okay, okay.

-Can we talk about
"Lizzie McGuire"?
My favorite.

-Fine, Jimmy.
-You are the greatest.

"Lizzie McGuire" was one
of my all-time faves,

and I was probably
way too old to love it.

But people love it,
and it holds up.

And you are so great.
-Love it!

-Yes!
[ Cheers and applause ]

But, Hilary Duff,
who was your Lizzie McGuire?

Who were you superfans of
when you were growing up?
-I mean, the Olsen twins.

-Yeah!
-Come on.
"Brother for Sale."

Brother for sale. Only 50 cents.
-Brother for sale.

I remember that.
-When they were spies.

They were like
twin-sister spies.
-They're so cute.

-They were like the --
I had it on video,

guys, my VHS, and I was obsessed
with the Olsen twins.

I -- I'm about to drop a lot
of names here.

Okay? I met the Olsen twins at
Aaron Carter's birthday party.

-What?
-Pre us dating, and I was like,
"I have arrived. I've made it.

Both of the Olsen twins
are here."

-You're like, "That's it.
You're both my favorite."

-Yeah. It was crazy.
-They are the coolest.

-But also, like, loved
"Clarissa Explains It All."

Loved Alex Mack
when she turned into the liquid

and, like,
floated under the door.

-Yes.
-Flying on like --

-"Clarissa Explains It All."
-Right?
-Yes. Melissa Joan Hart.

-Yeah.
-She's fantastic.

Well, everything on the Internet
obviously lives forever,

and people are going through
a photo shoot

that you did in your
"Lizzie McGuire" days

that was just confusing
to some people.

And it's --
-Yeah.

-And it's you
with a bunch of orange peels?

-Is that --
[ Laughter ]
-And no one can explain why.
-Is that strange for you?

-Yes.
Is that strange for me?

Yes.
-Okay. Yeah.

-Now, how does this make any
sense?

Do you remember this
and where were you and why?

-So it is wild that everything
lives forever on the Internet,
and I have grown up with --

Every phase that I have
gone through...

-Yes.
-...lives forever
on the Internet.

And I brought this up.
and I was like,

it's just unfortunate that,
like,

nobody else gets blamed
except for the person
that's, like, the subject.

-Yeah.
-'Cause who's gonna blame --
Who knows who took this picture?
No one cares.

-No.
-Who decided?

-Let's do a crazy one.
-Did a squirrel do this?

Did a squirrel do this
and we, like, came upon it,
and they're like, "Sit"?
-Look at this artwork, yeah.

-What is happening?
Were those for me?

Is it a snack?
I don't know. It's so strange.

Why am I --
This is called a child actor.
Like, what do you want me to do?

I'll do it.
Oranges on the floor.
Right? It's so concerning.

-Made me laugh.
I'm just -- Orange.

Just oranges tossed at you.
-Right.
-Just the oddest thing ever.

I thought it'd be fun if we
could answer some questions

that fans had because
we want to talk about the album,

first of all,
"Luck...or Something."

But we also have questions from
all of your fans

that have sent stuff
into "The Tonight Show."
-Every one of them?

-Yes,
every single one of your fans.

And I thought
if we could answer,

maybe three would be
kind of fun.

-Okay. Just --
-Okay. Question one.

Your favorite
on-screen love interest.
[ Cheers and applause ]
This is a buzzy question.
I know who I'm going to guess.

-Okay. I'm very curious to know
who you're going to guess.
-I'm going to -- I'm going to --

Circle.
-I don't want to look.

-Alright.
-I don't know.

Like, the audience
really just skewed my --
I'm just going to stick to it

because I just watched it
with my daughter.

And it's embarrassing that
she always wants to watch it.

I'm going to say
Chad Michael Murray.

-Wow!
[ Cheers and applause ]
He's a stud.
-It was the --

It was the walk
up the bleachers.

It was the slow walk
up the bleachers.

-It's a move, right?
-I know.

-It's the cutest thing.
-And the raindrop.

I was like, wow,
this is romantic.

-Alright. Good. I like that.
Question number two.

Favorite 2000s
Hilary Duff commercial.
Is it...

Is it the Disney Channel
commercial where you drew the --

Oh, wow!
Where you drew the ears,

or is it "Well, that's my life"
ad?

-Have you seen the slowed down
version of "That's my life"

where I look completely wasted?
-[ Laughs ] No.

But why would anyone do that
to you?

-It's so funny. I'm like,
"Well, that's my life."

-Oh, my God.
-Like 14 cocktails later.

She's not okay. Why is there
a belt around my neck?

What --
-What is happening?
I just -- It was a style.
-Yeah. Okay, I --
[ Laughter ]

Gosh, this is
a really, really tough one.

I think I'm going PSA.
-Really?

Yeah. I mean, what?
That thing took on so many legs.

I don't go anywhere
without someone being like,

"Thank you for that."
-Oh, really?

-It's so sweet.
-Alright. Good, I love that.

-That wasn't going to be
your choice.
-I liked just the Mickey ears.

Just you had a magic --
You were -- You're magical.

-Well, it was the -- it was
the not giving anything where,

you know -- Did we see this,
where I was like,

"I'm tired of this day"?
[ Laughter ]

-"I'm tired of this day."
-I'm tired of this day.

-Before we get to
question number three,

I want to talk to you
about your album and this tour.
Are you excited about this?
I mean, this is big deal.

You're playing two nights
at the Garden.

You're going all over the place.
[ Cheers and applause ]

It's a big deal.
-It's a really big deal.

-Do you get nervous before
you do these giant arenas?

-Well, I haven't done a giant
arena, Jimmy, in 20 years.

So, yeah, I'm going to be,
like, really nervous.

Really sweaty.
-Oh, my gosh.

The Lucky Me tour.
-The Lucky Me tour.
And I feel extremely lucky.

I think, you know,
being so far removed from music,

I haven't put an album
out in 10 years.
I didn't tour that record.
So this is like,

you just never know how
people are going to receive you.

-But people want it.
People loved it.

I mean, people, we were so happy
that you did it.

It was like it worked.
[ Cheers and applause ]

Like, yo, let's go, Hilary Duff,
we want it.
We want --
[ Cheers and applause continue ]
It's all great.
Also, I mean, we want more.

Is there another album
coming out?

What can we say?
Would you do more?
-Jimmy, take it easy.

-I'm sorry, I'm sorry,
I'm sorry.
-I've got four kids at home.

I still have to, you know,
divvy up my time.
No, I think -- of course.

I mean, I've lived with this
record for almost two years now,

so I'm really ready
for the next.

And there'll be a deluxe.
So we'll do that.

And, yeah, I'm in it now.
I'm in the cycle.

-Alright. Well, now knowing
this, here's my third question.

Which dance routine do
you still remember to this day?

Is it the one from
"Cadet Kelly"?
-No.

-Is it "What Dreams
Are Made Of"?

-No!
[ Cheers and applause ]
-Or is it "With Love"?

-Ding ding ding ding
ding ding ding!
-It's "With Love"?

-Yeah. Of course.
-I know that --

-You've resurrected
the "With Love" dance.

-I love that you did that,
though.
It was all over the place.
It blew up. It was super fun.

I was wondering
if you could maybe teach me
some moves from "With Love."

-Oh, my God. I would be honored.
[ Cheers and applause ]

-I would love it.
-Are you ready?

-Yes, please.
-Get up here.

-Alright.
-Let's go.

-Alright. Here we go.
How do we do this?
I'm excited.

-Okay. Are we --
You want me to slowly, like --

-Yeah.
Well, for me, definitely.

-Okay, we're gonna drag
your hand across your chest.

-Uh-huh.
-Then we're gonna sit.

We're driving the car, okay?
-Okay.

-Yeah. That's right.
-This is good.

-And we're going to go
foot, foot.

-Okay.
-And then --

-One, two.
-This one first.
Uh, uh.

Okay. And then you're gonna
knock your knees in.

-Oh, boy.
-There we go.

Now we're throwing our head
to the side.
Twist your arms up.
Twist your arms down.
Push your butt out.
Huh! Let's go uh-uh-uh-uh.

-Alright, let me try.
-That's enough, that's enough.

-Ready?
-Let's go.
Five, six. Let's go arms.
Six, seven, ha!
-I'm so off, I hurt myself.
Hilary Duff, everybody!

Here with the TV debut
of her song, "Roommates,"

from her new album
"Luck... or Something,"
once again, Hilary Duff!
[ Cheers and applause ]
